author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
# Findings

Removed the `if !op_view.touched_by_merge` guard at
`read.rs:85`, the `touched_by_merge` field on `OpView`, and its
setters. The cross-check then ran unconditionally and surfaced
a real divergence on the `pull_two_clones_same_field_lww_converges`
test: file had `"bob title"`, op-replay had `"alice title"`.

Diagnosis: the divergence was in `replay_ops` itself, not the
resolver. The old `replay_ops` walked `jj log` order (newest
→ oldest reversed), while the op-space resolver writes the
merged file by folding ops in spec §6 LWW total order
(`(jjf_at, commit, trailer_index)`). Across a merge those two
orderings pick different "winners" for the same field.

# Recommendation

Refactor `replay_ops` to fold via `history::read_history_at` +
a new `merge_ops::sort_entries_lww` helper that re-uses the
resolver's `OpKey`. File-read and op-replay now project the
same op chain identically by construction — the cross-check is
trivially valid across merges. Implemented in commit `f183a1c`.

# Confidence

High. All 232 workspace tests pass, including the
`pull_two_clones_*` LWW + disjoint-fields integration tests
that exercise the merge path with the cross-check live.
Regression test pinned in `merge_ops::tests::sort_entries_lww_orders_stamped_by_jjf_at`.

# Open follow-ups

- `trailer::parse_ops` is now `#[cfg(test)]`-only (fixture
  convenience). If a future caller wants typed ops without
  trailer meta, it can re-promote it.
- The doc-comment block at `read.rs:78-84` is updated; no
  forward-looking caveat remains. If a Jjf-Resolved-* trailer
  ever lands, it'd be a separate ticket.
